, PlotterBase , PolarToCartesian , PolarToComplex , PSNR , PythonScript , RecordAssembler , AtomicActor implements TypedActor A TypedAtomicActor is an AtomicActor whose ports and parameters have types. The typeConstraintList() method returns the typ

s. The typeConstraintList() method returns the type constraints among the contained ports and parameters. This base class provides a default implementation of this method, which should be suitable for most of the derived classes. Derived classes may

r most of the derived classes. Derived classes may constrain the container by overriding _checkContainer(). The Ports of TypedAtomicActors are constrained to be TypedIOPorts. Derived classes may further constrain the ports by overriding the public m

her constrain the ports by overriding the public method newPort() to create a port of the appropriate subclass, and the protected method _addPort() to throw an exception if its argument is a port that is not of the appropriate subclass. Since: Ptolem

is not of the appropriate subclass. Since: Ptolemy II 0.2 Version: $Id: TypedAtomicActor.java,v 1.63 2003/04/11 23:16:01 cxh Exp $ Author: Yuhong Xiong See Also: AtomicActor , TypedCompositeActor , TypedIOPort , Serialized Form Nested Class Summary

iteEntity container, java.lang.String name) throws IllegalActionException , NameDuplicationException Create a new actor in the specified container with the specified name. The name must be unique within the

he container. name - The name of this actor within the container. Throws: IllegalActionException - If this actor cannot be contained by the proposed container (see the setContainer() method). NameDuplicationException - If the name coincides with

uplicationException - If the name coincides with an entity already in the container. Method Detail attributeTypeChanged public void attributeTypeChanged ( Attribute attribute) throws IllegalActionException React to a chan

throws IllegalActionException React to a change in the type of an attribute. This method is called by a contained attribute when its type changes. In this base class, the method informs the director to invalidate type resolution, if the d

director to invalidate type resolution, if the director is not null. Thus, by default, attribute type changes cause type resolution to be redone at the next opportunity. If an actor does not allow attribute types to change, then it should o

llow attribute types to change, then it should override this method. Overrides: attributeTypeChanged in class NamedObj Parameters: attribute - The attribute whose type changed. Throws: IllegalActionException - Not thrown in this base class. Deri

micActor Parameters: name - The name for the new port. Returns: The new port. Throws: NameDuplicationException - If the actor already has a port with the specified name. See Also: Workspace.getWriteAccess() typeConstraintList public java.util.List

eAccess() typeConstraintList public java.util.List typeConstraintList () Return the type constraints of this actor. The constraints have the form of a list of inequalities. In this base class, if an input port and an output port do not have the

an input port and an output port do not have their types declared, and they do not have any constraints stored in them, a constraint is set up that requires the type of the input to be less than or equal to the type of the output. In addition

or equal to the type of the output. In addition, this method also collects type constraints from the contained Typeables (ports, variables, and parameters). This method is read-synchronized on the workspace. Specified by: typeConstraintList in

the workspace. Specified by: typeConstraintList in interface TypedActor Returns: A list of instances of Inequality. See Also: Inequality _addPort protected void _addPort ( Port port) throws IllegalActionException , NameDuplicationExc

throws IllegalActionException , NameDuplicationException Override the base class to throw an exception if the added port is not an instance of TypedIOPort. This method should not be used directly. Call the setContainer() method of the port inst

. Call the setContainer() method of the port instead. This method does not set the container of the port to point to this entity. It assumes that the port is in the same workspace as this actor. Derived classes may override this method to fu

. Derived classes may override this method to further constrain the port to be a subclass of TypedIOPort. This method is not synchronized on the workspace, so the caller should be. Overrides: _addPort in class AtomicActor Parameters: port - The p
